title: botrychium simplex subsp. simplex
intro: Botrychium simplex subsp. simplex, also known as the least grape fern, is a type of rare and endangered vascular plant belonging to the Osmundaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the least grape fern include its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be carefully considered and only done under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
